Metric Meaning: Orders per customer per day for new customers in the New Verticals segment.
Practical interpretation/Context: typically a tiebreaker metric capturing overall NV trends/ecosystem health. DbD and NV have a symbiotic relationship, with NV driving ~40% of DbD orders. if we're regressing this, chances are things are bad

Metric Meaning: Average number of support tickets filed by consumers per day.
Number of unique customers who placed at least one order in a given month.
Practical interpretation/Context: of major importance to the company. dealbreaker if we regress, allows us to claim company-wide impact (DD iGOV) if it increases, where we typically only claim O2 GOV

Metric Meaning: Number of customers who signed up for DashPass during the time period.
Practical interpretation/Context: sometimes doubledash, especially on post-checkout, redirects traffic to dashpass. this is usually a sign of app-wide performance spillover, and is almost never a consequence of direct design displacing/obscuring DP signup points on post-checkout

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers added a DoubleDash item after completing their initial order checkout.
Rate at which customers added a DoubleDash item either before or after their initial order checkout.
Rate at which customers added a DoubleDash item before completing their initial order checkout.
Average order value (subtotal only) for DoubleDash (O2) orders.
Practical interpretation/Context: this often increases in bad cases where we lose Cx on the margins (and thus only retain higher spending active Cx) and decreases when we acquire new, lower-spending Cx. not necessarily a tiebreaker in either direction

Metric Meaning: Total topline GMV from DoubleDash (O2) orders (calculated as order rate × GOV).
Practical interpretation/Context: ground truth metric. good for us if it's up. bad for us if not.

Metric Meaning: Subtotal (pre-fees/tips) for all DoubleDash (O2) items in an order.
Practical interpretation/Context: this often increases in bad cases where we lose Cx on the margins (and thus only retain higher spending active Cx) and decreases when we acquire new, lower-spending Cx. not necessarily a tiebreaker in either direction

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) trials per customer.
Practical interpretation/Context: important, but an input to order rate and gmv, which are at the top of our pyramid

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer per day.
Practical interpretation/Context: ground truth metric. good for us if it's up. bad for us if not.

Metric Meaning: Number of unique customers who placed at least one DoubleDash order in the last 28 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: important, but an input to order rate and gmv, which are at the top of our pyramid

Metric Meaning: Average number of orders per customer per day across DoorDash over the past 7 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: lower variance (will be more significant) than overall dbd_order_rate

Metric Meaning: Percentage of customers who trial a new merchant for them per day.
Practical interpretation/Context: interesting metric. typically we don't move it on dbd but when we do it's usually a sign of broad discovery occurring on O1 as a downstream effect of Doubledash.

Metric Meaning: Subtotal (pre-fees and tips) of the primary (O1) order that preceded a DoubleDash attachment.
Practical interpretation/Context: classic check metric, especially for pre-checkout doubledash. as more Cx use PCC, they typically leave some subtotal off of the O1. combined subtotal is the tiebreaker metric here.

Metric Meaning: Platform subtotal summed across all orders (excluding fees and tips).
Practical interpretation/Context: subtotal tiebreaker. good if up

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers opt out of account-level or system-level push notifications.
Discount value applied to customers through ad-driven promotions.
Revenue generated from in-app or platform advertisements.
Practical interpretation/Context: colloquially known as sponsored listing or ads revenue. rare if it moves, but will go down if we decrease overall app engagement

Metric Meaning: Average time in minutes for delivery assignment (DAT = delivery assignment time).
Practical interpretation/Context: amount of time a dasher is completing an order. good for understanding dasher cost results if batch rate hasn't moved.

Metric Meaning: Average number of orders per consumer over the last 28 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: overall order frequency on the app. not claimable for DD iGOV, but useful in understanding the contours of user behavior. also moves more significantly than other O1 indicators.

Metric Meaning: Time it takes to load a specific action page (e.g., home, search, checkout) in the Android app.
Practical interpretation/Context: app quality metric- app wide, the dimension values here matter a lot, as they'll speak to the specific action that was impacted. pay attention tto the dimension results.

Metric Meaning: Time it takes to load a specific action page (e.g., home, search, checkout) in the iOS app.
Practical interpretation/Context: app quality metric- app wide, the dimension values here matter a lot, as they'll speak to the specific action that was impacted. pay attention tto the dimension results.

Metric Meaning: Time it takes to load a specific action page (e.g., home, search, checkout) on the web app.
Practical interpretation/Context: app quality metric- app wide, the dimension values here matter a lot, as they'll speak to the specific action that was impacted. pay attention tto the dimension results.

Metric Meaning: Crash rate experienced by customers using the Android app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Crash rate experienced by customers using the iOS app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Crash rate experienced by customers using the web platform.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate of scroll performance issues experienced by customers on Android.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate of scroll performance issues experienced by customers on iOS.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Web performance metric measuring delay between a user interaction and next visual update.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate at which a page fails to respond as expected after customer action on the Android app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate at which a page fails to respond as expected after customer action on the iOS app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate at which a page fails to respond as expected after customer action on the web platform.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate of errors encountered while loading a page on the Android app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate of errors encountered while loading a page on the iOS app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Rate of errors encountered while loading a page on the web platform.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Time it takes for a page to fully load on the Android app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Time it takes for a page to fully load on the iOS app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Time it takes for a page to fully load on the web app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Time during which the main thread is blocked and unable to respond to user input on the web app.
Practical interpretation/Context: catchall app quality metric - something has to be pretty broken for our test to move this topline figure

Metric Meaning: Customers who paid their DashPass loyalty program fee balance.
Practical interpretation/Context: natural extension of dashpass_signups, which are explained above. that said, this is the tiebreaker/cumulative effect metric that captures paid balance and gives a better rounded view of dashpass effects

Metric Meaning: Average percentage of DoubleDash (O2) orders delivered more than 20 minutes late.
Practical interpretation/Context: keep an eye on this for logistics tests; it moves with batching and has a direct input to overall O2 high quality delivery rate (HQDR)

Metric Meaning: Average cancellation rate for DoubleDash (O2) orders.
Practical interpretation/Context: latent indicator of Cx satifaction; will go negative if wait time increases. not usually related to other dimensions of delivery quality

Metric Meaning: Average cost associated with the Dasher (courier) for DoubleDash (O2) orders.
Practical interpretation/Context: frequently comes up as the root cause of VP movements - particularly degradations - as dx cost is frequently changed by logistics/routing experiments

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from 1st-party convenience merchants (DashMart).
Practical interpretation/Context: these vertical specific metrics are tiebreakers/context metrics and an input to overall o2 gmv, not a dealbreaker if it goes negative nor is it a clear ship if it goes positive.

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from 3rd-party convenience merchants.
Practical interpretation/Context: these vertical specific metrics are tiebreakers/context metrics and an input to overall o2 gmv, not a dealbreaker if it goes negative nor is it a clear ship if it goes positive.

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from alcohol merchants.
Practical interpretation/Context: these vertical specific metrics are tiebreakers/context metrics and an input to overall o2 gmv, not a dealbreaker if it goes negative nor is it a clear ship if it goes positive.

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from flower merchants.
Practical interpretation/Context: these vertical specific metrics are tiebreakers/context metrics and an input to overall o2 gmv, not a dealbreaker if it goes negative nor is it a clear ship if it goes positive.

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from grocery merchants.
Practical interpretation/Context: these vertical specific metrics are tiebreakers/context metrics and an input to overall o2 gmv, not a dealbreaker if it goes negative nor is it a clear ship if it goes positive.

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from New Verticals merchants (includes 1pc, 3pc, alc, grocery, etc.).
Practical interpretation/Context: a more important vertical metric, but still a p1. NV drives ~40% of volume, most from 3PC

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from pet supply merchants.
Practical interpretation/Context: these vertical specific metrics are tiebreakers/context metrics and an input to overall o2 gmv, not a dealbreaker if it goes negative nor is it a clear ship if it goes positive.

Metric Meaning: Average Gross Order Value of DoubleDash (O2) orders from restaurant merchants.
Practical interpretation/Context: a more important vertical metric, but still a p1. Rx drives ~60% of volume

Metric Meaning: Average high-quality delivery rate for DoubleDash (O2) orders.
Practical interpretation/Context: super important - Cx have stated in research that concerns over delivery quality - specifically potential lateness/food quality - are meaningful in evaluating whether or not to order/reorder dbd

Metric Meaning: Average rate of missing or incorrect items in DoubleDash (O2) orders.
Average rate of DoubleDash (O2) orders that were not delivered.
Average rate at which Dashers accept both orders in a single tap to fulfill as one delivery (packaged orders).
Average rate of DoubleDash (O2) orders delivered as Same Dasher Batch (SDB).
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from 1st-party convenience merchants.
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from 3rd-party convenience merchants.
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from alcohol merchants.
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from flower merchants.
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from grocery merchants.
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from all New Verticals merchants.
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from pet supply merchants.
Average subtotal (pre-fees/tips) of DoubleDash (O2) orders from restaurant merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from 1st-party convenience merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from 3rd-party convenience merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from alcohol merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from flower merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from grocery merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from all New Verticals merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from pet supply merchants.
Average variable profit (VP) for DoubleDash (O2) orders from restaurant merchants.
Time it takes for the DoubleDash bottom sheet offer drawer to load on iOS.
Practical interpretation/Context: quality metric that typically has an inverse relationship with per-user conversion. if latency spikes, conversions decrease. hard to root cause this with metrics, usually points to an implementation gap/issue.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers come back and try DoubleDash again after 180 days without ordering.
Practical interpretation/Context: reactivation metric - there are a ton of trialists - as of 2/23/26, ~30% of DD WAU are DbD trialists that have been inactive for 180+ days

Metric Meaning: Rate at which existing (returning) customers added a DoubleDash item after completing their initial order checkout.
Rate of errors occurring during the DoubleDash checkout flow.
Rate at which customers click on a DoubleDash item in the funnel flow (e.g., after offer shown).
Rate at which customers load the DoubleDash checkout page.
Rate at which the DoubleDash store page successfully loads.
Rate of payment-related errors during the DoubleDash checkout flow.
Rate at which customers tap the 'Place Order' button in the DoubleDash funnel.
Average time between a customer being offered DoubleDash and placing a DoubleDash order.
Rate at which new (first-time) customers added a DoubleDash item after completing their initial order checkout.
Average number of net new New Verticals trials per customer (excluding repeats or retrials).
Average number of New Verticals trials per customer (including retrials).
Average number of New Verticals trial retrials per customer.
Average order value (subtotal only) for DoubleDash items added after the primary order checkout.
Total GMV from DoubleDash items added after the primary order checkout.
Average order value (subtotal only) for DoubleDash items added before the primary order checkout.
Total GMV from DoubleDash items added before the primary order checkout.
Total variable profit (VP) generated from DoubleDash (O2) orders.
Average variable profit (VP) generated per customer from DoubleDash (O2) orders.
Practical interpretation/Context: big focus in 2026 - not necessarily launch blocking if regressing, but definitely a focus point for DbD these days. the platform grew to where it is now on the strength of its $0 delivery fee, which creates a significant VP headwind. these days, we're averaging - 30 cents per order, and there are only two wayhs to claw this back : reduce costs (typically dasher costs) or increase order value

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) trials per customer where items were added after checkout.
Average number of DoubleDash (O2) trials per customer where items were added before checkout.
Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer over the full evaluation period.
Practical interpretation/Context: order rate is typically the driver of o2 gmv that we pay attention to, but order frequency is another driver. In general, order frequency increases reflect activity wins among active users. they're less impactful (& less common) in general, but still worth paying attention to.

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer in the past 14 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: lower variance order frequency metric. use the longest one  that's stat sig for anchoring analysis results

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer in the past 28 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: lower variance order frequency metric. use the longest one  that's stat sig for anchoring analysis results

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer in the past 7 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: lower variance order frequency metric. use the longest one  that's stat sig for anchoring analysis results

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer per day over the past 14 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: lower variance order rate metric. use the longest one  that's stat sig for actually claiming iGOV

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer per day over the past 28 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: lower variance order rate metric. use the longest one  that's stat sig for actually claiming iGOV

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer per day over the past 7 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: lower variance order rate metric. use the longest one  that's stat sig for actually claiming iGOV

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed New Verticals carousels on the post-checkout For-You page.
Practical interpretation/Context: browsing metrics - these are important, but often misleading. a well placed carousel in position 0 will regress this metric (and that would be a good thing!) conversely, worse recommendations at the top of the feed could drive the average user further down, but that would be a bad thing.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed Restaurant carousels on the post-checkout For-You page.
Practical interpretation/Context: browsing metrics - these are important, but often misleading. a well placed carousel in position 0 will regress this metric (and that would be a good thing!) conversely, worse recommendations at the top of the feed could drive the average user further down, but that would be a bad thing.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed Restaurant items on the post-checkout For-You page.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed Restaurant stores on the post-checkout For-You page.
Practical interpretation/Context: browsing metrics - these are important, but often misleading. a well placed carousel in position 0 will regress this metric (and that would be a good thing!) conversely, worse recommendations at the top of the feed could drive the average user further down, but that would be a bad thing.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed New Verticals stores via the nav bar on the post-checkout page.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed Restaurant stores via the nav bar on the post-checkout page.
Practical interpretation/Context: browsing metrics - these are important, but often misleading. a well placed carousel in position 0 will regress this metric (and that would be a good thing!) conversely, worse recommendations at the top of the feed could drive the average user further down, but that would be a bad thing.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed New Verticals stores on the post-checkout page.
Practical interpretation/Context: browsing metrics - these are important, but often misleading. a well placed carousel in position 0 will regress this metric (and that would be a good thing!) conversely, worse recommendations at the top of the feed could drive the average user further down, but that would be a bad thing.

Metric Meaning: Rate at which customers browsed Restaurant stores on the post-checkout page.

Metric Meaning: Average number of New Verticals items a customer browsed within a single carousel through horizontal scroll depth on the post-checkout For-You page.

Metric Meaning: Average number of Restaurant items a customer browsed within a single carousel through horizontal scroll depth on the post-checkout For-You page.

Metric Meaning: Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer per day after the primary order checkout.
Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer per day after checkout over the past 14 days.
Average number of DoubleDash (O2) orders per customer per day after checkout over the past 28 days.

Customer who placed at least one DoubleDash order within the evaluation period.
Practical interpretation/Context: also called pMAU, this is a super useful topline customer acquisition metric. # cx placing doubledash order/ #cx placing any order

Metric Meaning: Number of unique customers who placed a DoubleDash order post-checkout in the last 28 days.
Number of unique customers who placed a DoubleDash order pre-checkout in the last 28 days.
Customer who placed at least one DoubleDash order post-checkout during the evaluation period.
Number of unique customers who placed at least one DoubleDash order post-checkout in the past 7 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: post-checkout pMAU. lower variance than topline

Metric Meaning: Customer who placed at least one DoubleDash order pre-checkout during the evaluation period.
Number of unique customers who placed at least one DoubleDash order pre-checkout in the past 7 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: pre-checkout pMAU. lower variance than topline

Metric Meaning: Number of unique customers who placed at least one DoubleDash order in the past 7 days.
Rate at which DashPass subscribers paused or canceled their membership.
Average number of orders per customer per day across DoorDash over the past 14 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: windowed core dd order rate metric, untouchable check metric

Metric Meaning: Average number of orders per customer per day across DoorDash over the past 28 days.
Practical interpretation/Context: windowed core dd order rate metric, untouchable check metric

Metric Meaning: Percentage of customer support tickets resolved on the first contact without requiring additional follow-up.
Gross order value per order for the Curie cohort or experimental group.
Version 2 metric estimating the long-term relevance of homepage session outcomes (e.g., purchase likelihood, engagement).
Ratio of deliveries meeting the high-quality delivery criteria vs. all deliveries.
Practical interpretation/Context: a concern for dbd given batching, lateness risks associated with batching

Metric Meaning: Average merchant take-home pay in Mexico over the past 7 days.
Net revenue earned by DoorDash per order, after subtracting costs, promotions, and incentives.
Number of customers who placed their first completed New Verticals order (across all verticals).
Rate at which New Verticals orders are fulfilled without issues (e.g., on-time, complete, accurate).
Number of unique customers who placed at least one New Verticals order in the past month.
Practical interpretation/Context: untouchable adjacent team metric

Metric Meaning: Average order value of the primary (O1) order placed before a DoubleDash attachment.
Practical interpretation/Context: pay attention to this for PCC tests, given previously mentioned cart building interference. not launch blocking if this regresses, but ox subtotal combined must be solidly neutral+

Metric Meaning: Gross merchandise value (GMV) of the primary (O1) order that preceded a DoubleDash attachment.
Practical interpretation/Context: pay attention to this for PCC tests, given previously mentioned cart building interference. not launch blocking if this regresses, but ox subtotal combined must be solidly neutral+

Metric Meaning: Total variable profit generated from the primary (O1) order before the DoubleDash attachment.
Average variable profit per customer from primary (O1) orders preceding a DoubleDash.
Rate at which total orders increase through merchants with high item-level price inflation.
Average number of orders per customer per day over the past 7 days.
Order volume from small and medium-sized restaurant businesses.
Variable profit (VP) earned per individual order.
Order volume generated from customers with over 25 lifetime orders.
important guardrail - this is the official company wide order rate metric
Practical interpretation/Context: p0 order rate guardrail

Metric Meaning: important guardrail - this WAS the official company wide order rate metric. dsmp = discovery, search, merchandising, personalization
Practical interpretation/Context: p0 order rate guardrail - also very important

Metric Meaning: company wide pMAU (% of DD visitors ordering anything O1 or O2 on DD)
